observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to: a) To exert efforts in reconciling the bank and book balances and record valid reconciling items; b) Reconcile the balances per subsidiary ledgers and timely update it for the transactions of the LGU; and c) Prepare and submit a complete and timely bank reconciliation statements and reconcile the same with the balances reported in the Financial Statements

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the LGU to require the concerned AO to immediately settle or refund the CAs which are already due for liquidation, especially those who have long outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2024;
b. Initiate appropriate measures, including the immediate withholding of salaries and other unpaid claims due for the erring AO who are still unable to settle their accounts after demand; and
c. Maintain updated general and subsidiary ledgers to aid in tracking and reconciling of cash advances and ensure proper safekeeping of supporting documents to aid in reconciliation of balances.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the guidelines provided in COA Circular 2024-006 in recording the acquisitions and issuances of the semi-expendable properties.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare updated lapsing schedules and complete subsidiary/general ledgers with prior years transactions to support the balances of PPE and accumulated depreciation reported in the financial statements and to serve as a valid basis for the computation of depreciation expense; and
b. Direct the Accounting Office to locate the supporting documents, reconcile and validate PPE balances in the previous years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounts payable balance between the FS and SL and ensure that the correct and accurate balance supported with schedules are presented in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due to LGUs account, present a breakdown of the balances presented in the financial statements, and prepare necessary adjusting entries, if applicable.
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the remaining funds subject to return/remit to the respective agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance per Financial Statements and balances per subsidiary ledgers of Due to BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth, and HDMF each month to ensure the accuracy of the Financial Statements and correctness of the amounts of remittances due and avoid penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Require the Local Engineer and/or concerned authorized agency personnel mandated to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects to submit to the audit team copies of the Results of Test Analyses/Status of Quality Control Test required by the aforementioned rules and regulations in order to ensure that the completed projects conforms with agency standards, plans and specifications as part of its quality control program.
b. Require the Local Engineer/BAC to include Minimum Materials Testing Equipment in the Technical Component of the Bid, together with the other major equipment as contained in the Technical Envelope, which shall be subjected to preliminary examination and further verified during the post-qualification stage.
c. Henceforth, require the Local Accountant not to pass during pre-audit of the transaction particularly on final payment, disbursement vouchers on infrastructure projects not supported with Results of Test Analyses from DPWH/BRS/DOST/DTI accredited testing laboratories for construction materials and concrete which requires testing to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Engineer to implement a sound and effective monitoring system in place to compel contractor’s compliance of the requirements of Section 62 of the RIRR of RA 9184 in order to ensure that the procuring entity will be reimbursed of its expenses in the restoration or reconstruction works in case of structural defect/failure occuring during the applicable warranty period. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor of the above-mentioned project to submit warranty security

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to require the concerned contractors to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the posting of the project signboard to ensure full transparency and accountability in the implementation of the infrastructure projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due to NGAs account, present a breakdown of the balances presented in the financial statements, and prepare necessary adjusting entries, if applicable.
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the remaining funds subject to return to the source agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO together with the Barangay Officials, if any, to submit the complete distribution list and consumption report immediately to the Team for validation and audit.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the sources, utilization, and unexpended balance of the DRRM Fund in the Notes to FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the:
a. Municipal accountant to prepare BRS for all fund accounts on a regular
basis.
b. Municipal Accountant to identify the details of the reconciling items
especially the long outstanding ones and prepare the necessary
accounting/adjusting entries and revert back to the appropriate funds the
stale and cancelled checks and establish the liability of the LGU; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation
of cashbook, accounting records and bank balances on a regular basis.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of
all PPE and prepare RCPPE with complete details;
b) Require the Property Custodian Office to maintain individual property
card for all PPE owned by the LGU;
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPELC to ensure proper
monitoring;
d) Instruct the Accounting and Property Office to investigate and identify
the noted differences between the Accounting records and RCPPE that
may require adjustment/s; and
e) Undertake the necessary action/s for the disposal of the unserviceable
properties to avoid further deterioration and consequent depreciation in
its value.

We recommended that Management:
1. Submit the complete Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
System using the format in Annex C of the Circular;
2. Require the Property Custodian to: a) prepare a Report on Local Road
Network as required in Annex A; b) maintain local road inventory and
road map; and c) maintain property cards for all roads and its
components;
3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) prepare lapsing schedule
showing the computation of the depreciation of each component of the
Road Network System account; and b) disclose the total Road Network
System account in the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed in
Annex B of the said Circular; and
4. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the designated Property
Custodian and Municipal Accountant with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components for a road project.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Verify and reconcile the difference between SL and GL;
b. Fast track the reconciliation of the said accounts; and
c. Settle immediately the unremitted taxes withheld to BIR and
premiums/loans amortizations deducted from the salaries of the
employees to GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth to avoid any sanctions that
may be imposed due to late or non-remittance thereof.

We recommended that the Management: a) secure and attach the complete
supporting documents prior to recording of payable accounts to the general journal;
and b) regularly conduct clean-up of Accounts Payable specially those payables that
have been in record beyond 1 year.

We recommended that Management:
a. implement the programs/projects promptly using the fund transfers received
from various government agencies, if any;
b. submit all the documents requested by the Audit Team related to these fund
transfers; and
c. review all the unexpended balances of funds received and immediately return
any unused/unexpended balances to the National Treasury or revert it to
Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund, if any.

We recommended that the Management:
a. Insure all of their properties with the GSIS GIF to avoid exposure from
unnecessary risk; and
b. Prepare and submit the PIF listing of all insurable assets showing their
latest appraised values/valuation, appraisal date, location, and other
information.

We recommended the Management to:
1) Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference of the
unexpended balances reported in the Notes to Financial Statements and
LDRRMF Utilization Report;
2) Transfer the reconciled unexpended balance to Trust Liabilities-
MDRRMF account under the Trust Fund;
3) Prepare the Subsidiary Ledgers as required under Section 5.1.12 of
Circular No. 2012-002; and
4) Submit the distribution lists.

We recommended that the Management submit the required liquidation and
financial reports to DSWD Region V, copy furnished our Office, for monitoring of
the SAP fund.

We recommended that the Management:
(a) Submit the Skeletal Workforce (SKF) schedule, memoranda or office
orders specifically identifying who among the personnel were allowed and
authorized to physically report for work during ECQ;
(b) Furnish us a duly filled out DTRs; and
(c) Provide a valid justification on all discrepancies noted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that Management: a.) require the concerned elective/municipal officials and employees and Job Order Workers who are still in the service to settle immediately their outstanding cash advances, else deduct from their salaries the unliquidated cash advances; b.) stop the granting of additional cash advances unless previous cash advances had been liquidated; c.) send final demand letters to persons previously connected with the Municipality who still have unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, officials responsible for clearing said individuals from money accountability be held liable for the unsettled cash advances; d.) instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the grant of cash advances and enforce strictly the immediate liquidation thereof; e.) request for authority to write-off the unliquidated cash advances especially those pertaining to deceased accountable officers; and f.) instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry on erroneous recording of receivable accounts and ensure that cash advances are recorded using the appropriate receivable account.

We recommended that Management: a) require the Inventory Committee to conduct the regular physical count of all properties with the prescribed report and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of each year; b) ensure that all properties of the LGU are provided with tag/s or sticker/s that contain the relevant specification and property number of the asset; and c) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Property Card and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card to ensure proper monitoring.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to validate the reliability of the recorded balances and refund the unused fund in order that the account of the Municipality in the books of the source agency is settled.

We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Assessor prioritize the updating of its records particularly the Field Appraisal & Assessment Sheet (FAAS) of all real properties in the Municipality so that correct amount of real property taxes may be assessed and/or collected.

We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Accountant to: a) prepare the breakdown/details of the unutilized amount per year unexpended balances’ of LDRRMF and disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements; b) maintain subsidiary ledgers per year of transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF; c) include all the information needed in the preparation of the MDRRMF Utilization Report; d) not to process transactions with inadequate documentations; e) follow the prescribed accounting guideline and entries in recording transactions charged to LDRRMF; and f) prepare the necessary adjusting entries;
instruct the LDRRM Officer to: a) observe proper safekeeping of supplies, tools and equipment under the custody of the MDRRM Office and maintain an updated inventory list on a regular basis; b) turn over/transfer the safekeeping and distribution of medicines under his custody to the Rural Health Unit (RHU); c) submit the lacking documents required in the disbursement of LDRRMF under the QRF amounting to ₱320,000 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension; and d) stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials or services through reimbursement basis and ensure that the procurement process was in accordance with RA No. 9184; and
require the refund of the total amount of ₱15,466.17 to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that Management cause the persons responsible for the acceptance of the bid resulting to the approval of the contract to pay the amount of ₱77,593.33 representing the difference between the billboard cost per contract and the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).
